title: Stipagrostis brachyathera
taxonomy: Stipagrostis brachyathera is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Stipagrostis, which is a group of small to medium-sized grasses commonly found in semi-arid to arid regions. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots that includes many different types of grasses. Within the family Poaceae, Stipagrostis brachyathera is classified as a member of the subfamily Chloridoideae. This subfamily is characterized by grasses with spikelets and a two-flowered spikelet.

intro: Stipagrostis brachyathera, also known as the short-grass stips or short-grass grader, is a species of grass native to Southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stipagrostis brachyathera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
